creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to shamaz.mazum from comment #12)
> Is there a rule that I must avoid post-patch target?
There's no hard rule, but naturally passing variables down via MAKE_ARGS is preferred to patching files with sed(1).

>  And what does that mean "CMake is deprecated upstream"?
From the CMakeLists.txt:
# Print a message that using the Makefiles is recommended.
message(NOTICE: " The CMakeLists.txt is unmaintained. Use the Makefile if possible.")
